title: The Art of Thinking Clearly
subtitle: A catalog of cognitive biases and logical fallacies that distort human judgment, with practical advice for avoiding them.
author: Rolf Dobelli
themes: The systematic errors in human judgment
Why smart people make dumb decisions
The availability heuristic and what comes easily to mind
Confirmation bias and the search for confirming evidence
Overconfidence and the illusion of knowledge
Social biases — herd instinct, authority bias, and the endowment effect
awards: International bestseller (over 3 million copies sold worldwide)
excerpt: Why do we consistently make the same thinking errors — and how can we stop? Rolf Dobelli catalogs 99 cognitive biases and logical fallacies that distort human judgment, from confirmation bias and the sunk cost fallacy to the halo effect and the spotlight effect. Each chapter is a short, digestible essay on a single bias, drawn from research in behavioral economics, cognitive psychology, and neuroscience.
